justification.personality: The book portrays her as a knowledgeable, supportive friend who speaks up during the hearing. The film only shows her in a brief, non-speaking appearance during the sorting, limiting any personality assessment.
justification.narrative_role: In the books, she plays a minor but supportive role in the climax of the Ministry hearing. In the film, her role is reduced to a single, unmemorable sorting moment.
justification.motivations: Her primary motivation in the books is supporting Harry and speaking truth to power during the hearing. This is not reflected in the film.
justification.character_arc: The book gives her a moment of quiet support and participation in the main plot. The film gives her no discernible arc, merely a single appearance.

comparative.key_observations: Susan Bones is a character whose presence is significantly diminished in the film adaptation compared to the source material, resulting in low scores across the board.
